description: Set up production-grade build tooling for the docs/ standalone project to match the same high standard as the root monorepo. This includes ESLint (flat config, strict TypeScript-aware rules), Prettier (identical formatting), Knip (unused code detection), type-check scripts, and fixing all issues found by these tools.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Install all tooling devDependencies and add package.json scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs project to have all the same tooling dependencies as the root so that I can run lint, format, type-check, and knip commands independently.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/package.json has devDependencies for: eslint, typescript-eslint, @eslint/js, @eslint/compat, @eslint/eslintrc, eslint-config-prettier, eslint-plugin-prettier, eslint-plugin-import-x, eslint-import-resolver-typescript, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, eslint-plugin-react, eslint-plugin-react-hooks, globals, prettier, prettier-plugin-tailwindcss, knip
docs/package.json scripts include: type-check, lint, lint:fix, format, format:check, knip
type-check script runs: tsc --noEmit
lint script runs: eslint .
lint:fix script runs: eslint . --fix
format script runs: prettier --write --cache "**/*.{ts,tsx,json,md,mjs}"
format:check script runs: prettier --check "**/*.{ts,tsx,json,md,mjs}"
knip script runs: knip
bun install succeeds in docs/ directory
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/package.json to add devDependencies and scripts. Use the same dependency versions as the root package.json where possible. The root uses: eslint ^9.39.2, typescript-eslint ^8.56.0, prettier ^3.8.1, knip ^5.83.1, etc. After editing package.json, run 'bun install' in docs/. The docs project uses Next.js 16 with React 19 and Tailwind CSS 4. Keep existing dependencies and scripts intact — only add new ones. IMPORTANT: Do NOT add husky or lint-staged — those belong at the root level only. The format script should include .mjs files since docs has next.config.mjs and postcss.config.mjs. Also add @typescript-eslint/parser as a devDependency (required peer dep for typescript-eslint). Make sure to also keep the existing 'dev', 'build', 'start', 'preview' scripts.

userStories.title: Create ESLint flat config for docs project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs project to have an ESLint configuration that matches the root's strict standards, adapted for a Next.js/Fumadocs project.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/eslint.config.ts exists and uses ESLint 9 flat config format
Uses typescript-eslint strictTypeChecked preset with projectService: true
Includes eslint-plugin-import-x with same import ordering rules as root
Includes eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y recommended
Includes eslint-plugin-react recommended + jsx-runtime
Includes eslint-plugin-react-hooks recommended (scoped to .tsx files)
Includes eslint-plugin-prettier recommended

Has same rules as root: eqeqeq, prefer-const, no-var, func-style expression, arrow-body-style as-needed, consistent-type-imports, consistent-type-exports, restrict-template-expressions, no-confusing-void-expression
Has import-x/order with same settings as root (no newlines between, alphabetize asc, groups)
Ignores: .next/, .source/, out/, node_modules/, next-env.d.ts
Has @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars with underscore ignore patterns matching root
reportUnusedDisableDirectives is set to error
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create docs/eslint.config.ts modeled after the root eslint.config.ts. Key differences from root: (1) No need for browser-extension or mcp-server specific overrides — all files run in Node/browser Next.js context. (2) Ignore .next/, .source/, out/ instead of dist/build. (3) Add 'next-env.d.ts' to ignores since it's auto-generated. (4) Use browser + node globals since Next.js code runs in both. (5) The docs project has source.config.ts (a fumadocs config file at root level) that should be included. The root eslint config structure to follow: js.configs.recommended -> tseslintConfigs.strictTypeChecked -> jsxA11y.flatConfigs.recommended -> importXFlatConfig.recommended -> importXFlatConfig.typescript -> eslintPluginPrettierRecommended -> react-hooks (scoped to .tsx) -> react recommended + jsx-runtime (scoped to .ts/.tsx). Make sure the config file itself is valid TypeScript and can be imported by eslint.

userStories.title: Create Prettier config and Knip config for docs project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs project to have identical Prettier formatting rules and Knip unused code detection matching the root project standards.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/.prettierrc exists with identical settings to root: trailingComma all, semi true, singleQuote true, arrowParens avoid, printWidth 120, bracketSameLine true, htmlWhitespaceSensitivity strict, plugins prettier-plugin-tailwindcss
docs/.prettierignore exists ignoring: .next, .source, out, node_modules, bun.lock
docs/knip.ts exists with appropriate config for a Next.js fumadocs project

Knip config has correct entry points: app/layout.tsx, app/page.tsx, app/docs/layout.tsx, app/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx, app/api/search/route.ts, source.config.ts, mdx-components.tsx, next.config.mjs, postcss.config.mjs
Knip config ignores auto-generated files: .next/, .source/, out/
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Create three files: (1) docs/.prettierrc — exact copy of root .prettierrc. (2) docs/.prettierignore — adapted for docs project (ignore .next, .source, out, node_modules, bun.lock). (3) docs/knip.ts — configure for Next.js project. Knip needs to know the entry points. For Next.js: app/layout.tsx is the root layout, app/page.tsx is the homepage, app/docs/layout.tsx and app/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx are the docs pages. Also source.config.ts (fumadocs config), mdx-components.tsx (Next.js MDX components), next.config.mjs, postcss.config.mjs are entry points. The components/ directory files are imported from the page/layout files or mdx-components.tsx. The lib/ files are imported from app/ files. Use KnipConfig type import from 'knip'. Set ignore to ['**/.next/**', '**/.source/**', '**/out/**']. May need to set ignoreDependencies for fumadocs packages or Next.js internals that knip can't trace. The knip config must be valid TypeScript with proper type annotation.

userStories.title: Strengthen tsconfig.json to match root strictness flags
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs tsconfig.json to include the same strict TypeScript flags as the root project for consistent code quality.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/tsconfig.json has noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true
docs/tsconfig.json has noFallthroughCasesInSwitch: true
docs/tsconfig.json has noImplicitReturns: true
docs/tsconfig.json has noImplicitOverride: true
Existing tsconfig settings are preserved (paths, plugins, include, jsx, etc.)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes (or errors are documented for US-005)
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/tsconfig.json compilerOptions to add the four additional strict flags from the root tsconfig: noUncheckedIndexedAccess, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch, noImplicitReturns, noImplicitOverride. These are all set to true. Keep all existing settings intact. The existing tsconfig already has strict: true, which covers strictNullChecks, strictFunctionTypes, etc. These four additional flags add extra strictness that the root enforces. After adding these flags, run 'cd docs && bun run type-check' to see if any new errors appear. If errors appear, they will be fixed in US-005.

userStories.title: Fix all TypeScript type-check errors in docs project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all TypeScript errors in the docs project to be resolved so that bun run type-check passes cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: cd docs && bun run type-check exits with code 0
No type errors remain in any .ts or .tsx file
Fixes use proper typing (no @ts-ignore, no 'any' type assertions unless absolutely unavoidable)
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Run 'cd docs && bun run type-check' and fix all errors. Common issues in Next.js fumadocs projects: (1) Missing type imports for fumadocs types. (2) Implicit any in component props — add proper interfaces. (3) noUncheckedIndexedAccess may cause issues with array/object access — add null checks or non-null assertions where safe. (4) Component prop types may need updating for React 19 patterns. (5) The mdx-components.tsx file maps MDX components — check its types carefully. (6) lib/utils.ts imports ClassNameValue from tailwind-merge — verify import is correct. (7) source.config.ts uses fumadocs-mdx/config imports. If there are many errors, fix them methodically file by file. Prioritize proper fixes over type assertions.

userStories.title: Fix all ESLint errors in docs project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all ESLint errors in the docs project to be resolved so that bun run lint passes cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: cd docs && bun run lint exits with code 0
No eslint-disable comments are used
All import ordering follows the configured rules
All files use consistent-type-imports where applicable
func-style expression rule is satisfied (no function declarations, use const arrow functions)
arrow-body-style as-needed is satisfied
eqeqeq is satisfied (no == or !=)
prefer-const is satisfied
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Run 'cd docs && bun run lint' and fix all errors. Start with 'bun run lint:fix' to auto-fix what's possible, then manually fix the rest. Common issues to expect: (1) Import ordering — imports need to be reordered per import-x/order rules. (2) Missing 'type' keyword on type-only imports (consistent-type-imports). (3) Function declarations need to be converted to const arrow functions (func-style expression). (4) Arrow functions with block bodies that could be expressions (arrow-body-style as-needed). (5) == instead of === (eqeqeq). (6) let that should be const (prefer-const). (7) Unused variables (no-unused-vars). (8) React-specific issues. (9) jsx-a11y accessibility issues. (10) TypeScript strict type-checked rules may flag unsafe operations. IMPORTANT: The RetroUI components in components/retroui/ are likely the largest source of issues — there are 30+ component files. Fix them systematically. If eslint config itself has issues (e.g., parser errors), fix the config first. CRITICAL: Never use eslint-disable comments. Fix the actual code. If a third-party type causes an unavoidable lint issue, the correct fix is to properly type the code, not suppress the warning.

userStories.title: Fix all Prettier formatting issues in docs project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all files in the docs project to be consistently formatted according to Prettier rules matching the root project.
acceptanceCriteria: cd docs && bun run format:check exits with code 0
All .ts, .tsx, .json, .md, .mjs files are properly formatted
Single quotes used everywhere
Trailing commas present
120 char print width respected
Arrow parens avoided for single params
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Run 'cd docs && bun run format' to auto-format all files. Then verify with 'bun run format:check'. Prettier auto-fix should handle most formatting. After formatting, re-run lint and type-check to make sure formatting changes didn't break anything (they shouldn't, since we use eslint-config-prettier to disable conflicting rules). If any files fail format:check after running format, investigate why — it could be a malformed file or a prettier parser issue.

userStories.title: Fix all Knip unused code issues in docs project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all unused dependencies, exports, and files detected by Knip to be resolved in the docs project.
acceptanceCriteria: cd docs && bun run knip exits with code 0
No unused dependencies remain (either remove them or add to ignoreDependencies if they are runtime/framework dependencies that Knip can't trace)
No unused exports remain (either remove them or ensure they are actually used)
No unused files remain
Any ignoreDependencies additions in knip.ts are justified with comments
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Run 'cd docs && bun run knip' and address all issues. Common Knip issues in Next.js fumadocs projects: (1) Unused dependencies — fumadocs may have peer deps that aren't directly imported but are required at runtime. Radix UI components may be used only via re-exports. (2) Unused exports — the RetroUI barrel export (components/retroui/index.ts) re-exports everything, but not all may be used. Either remove unused re-exports or add ignoreExportsUsedInFile if they're part of a public component library. (3) Unused files — check if any component files are truly unused. (4) Next.js framework dependencies like postcss, @tailwindcss/postcss may be flagged as unused since they're referenced in config files not source code. Add these to ignoreDependencies with a comment explaining why. (5) The knip.ts config may need entry points adjusted based on what knip reports. (6) @types/ packages are dev-time only and may be flagged. (7) Some fumadocs plugins may need to be in ignoreDependencies. Prefer removing truly unused code over ignoring it. Only use ignoreDependencies as a last resort for framework/build-time dependencies that Knip genuinely cannot trace.
